hero.question:
Your AI agent just mass-deleted
your production code.
Who has the proof?
hero.subtitle: Every AI action gets a receipt.
hero.tagline: Dashcam for AI coding. Append-only, Ed25519-signed, RFC 3161 timestamped.

why.title: // Why I built this
why.story: I've been using Claude Code daily since January 2025. After 25,000+ AI actions, I realized I had zero proof of what actually happened. The AI's own logs? It can edit those. Git history? Doesn't capture the thinking process.
why.punchline: I wanted something that works like a dashcam — always recording, can't be tampered with, and there when you need it.
why.audience: If you use Claude Code or Cursor for client work, team projects, or anything where you need to show what your AI actually did — this is for you.

trust.title: // Three Trust Layers
trust.subtitle: Each layer adds a guarantee the layer below cannot provide.
trust.merkle_desc: Proves this event is in the tree, and the tree is append-only. RFC 6962.
trust.ed25519_desc: Proves this kernel instance signed this checkpoint. RFC 8032.
trust.tsa_desc: Proves this checkpoint existed before time T. You cannot backdate it. RFC 3161.

tools.title: // Supported Tools
tools.claude_code: Claude Code — 10 hooks (tools, sessions, subagents, notifications) + statusline
tools.mcp: MCP Server — 7 tools for agent self-query

philosophy.title: // Right to History
philosophy.definition: The Right to History means: an actor spends their own energy to initiate an action, writes the outcome into verifiable world state, and leaves an indelible trace.
philosophy.p_desc: Human or Agent. Humans exist unconditionally; agents exist conditionally.
philosophy.e_desc: Universal cost metric for actions. Anchored to hardware INT8 TOPS, not arbitrary tokens.
philosophy.s_desc: All persistent reality and verifiable history. S' = f(S, A, E).
philosophy.formula: The system advances by action, not by time.
philosophy.committer: The system must have a single committer — a structural role, not a moral authority.

steps.validate_desc: Check actor status, boundary permissions, action format
steps.quote_desc: Compute energy cost of the action
steps.settle_desc: Finalize energy accounting incl. I/O overhead
steps.append_desc: Write to append-only log + cryptographic hash

invariants.title: Single Committer
invariants.desc: One commit authority per history domain

invariants.title: Total Order Log
invariants.desc: Committed events form a single total-order sequence

invariants.title: State Reconstructability
invariants.desc: Current state derivable by replaying events

invariants.title: Semantic Append-Only
invariants.desc: Committed events cannot be rewritten, only compensated

invariants.title: Governance Auditable
invariants.desc: Governance rules and changes enter event history

invariants.title: Evidence Independently Verifiable
invariants.desc: Third parties can verify history integrity

showcase_entry.title: ENTER THE LAB
showcase_entry.subtitle: See what's being built with PunkGo Kernel

showcase.title: // Built with PunkGo
showcase.subtitle: Projects forged on the kernel. Each one writes its own history.

watchdog.name: WATCHDOG
watchdog.desc: Creation process provenance. Watches your files, submits every change to the kernel's append-only log with Merkle proofs. Not just proving a file exists — proving when and how it came into being.

jack.name: JACK
jack.desc: AI tool hook adapter. Every tool call from any AI coding assistant is automatically captured and committed to the kernel with a cryptographic receipt. Session tracking, energy accounting, and a terminal heatmap of your coding activity. Not just using AI — proving what it did.

whitepaper.title: // PunkGo Whitepaper
whitepaper.epigraph: When AI does everything for you, who proves you still exist?
whitepaper.intro: PunkGo is a local sovereignty compute kernel: giving individuals the inalienable right to write history on their own hardware.
whitepaper.s1_body: An actor spends its own energy to initiate actions, commits results to verifiable world state, and leaves an indelible trace.
whitepaper.s2_intro: The system is composed of three primitive objects:
whitepaper.s2_e_desc: Energy, the universal cost metric for actions
whitepaper.s2_s_desc: State, all persistent reality and history, including the verifiable structure of how history was written
whitepaper.s2_advance: The system advances by action, not by time:
whitepaper.s2_actions: Where A is an atomic action initiated by P: observe (read), create (create), mutate (modify), execute (execute, must be encapsulated with recordable input/output).
whitepaper.s2_committer: The system MUST have a unique Committer — a structural role, not a moral authority — responsible for turning Actions into committed events, providing a single linearization commit point.

s3_items.title: Single Committer
s3_items.desc: A single history domain has exactly one commit authority

s3_items.title: Total Order Log
s3_items.desc: Committed events form a single total-order sequence

s3_items.title: State Reconstructability
s3_items.desc: S can be derived by replaying events

s3_items.title: Semantic Append-Only
s3_items.desc: Committed events cannot be rewritten; corrections are made only by appending compensating events

s3_items.title: Provable Append-Only
s3_items.desc: The append-only property of history can be independently verified

s3_items.title: Governance Auditable
s3_items.desc: Governance rules and their changes enter the event history

s3_items.title: Evidence Independently Verifiable
s3_items.desc: Third parties can independently verify history integrity

roast.title: What kind of dog is your AI?
roast.subtitle: PunkGo Roast analyzes your real AI coding sessions and tells you which of 16 dog personalities matches your style. No quiz. No self-report. Just your actual data.
roast.install_note: Takes 30 seconds. Nothing leaves your machine.
roast.step2:
It reads your AI session history
(local only)
roast.step3:
You get your card.
Share it or don't.
roast.brand_footer: Roast is powered by {link} — a dashcam for AI coding.
